items.text: Multi-source harmonization steps and remaining incompatibilities are described.
Processing code regenerates the modeling dataset from raw sources; dataset equivalence checks are stated.
All input features/descriptors are defined with their generation pipeline.
Dataset identifiers, access details, and core metadata for reuse are provided.
Policy on negative/failed/suboptimal experiments is stated; informative negatives are included when possible.
Full model specification (architecture, hyperparameters, objectives, versions/checkpoints) is documented.
Data-split strategy prevents information leakage; split rules and seeds are reported.
Performance metrics with confidence intervals are reported to avoid best-only results.
Metric choice and multi-objective trade-offs are justified (e.g., Pareto fronts or weighting).
Uncertainty quantification method, calibration, and how UQ is used in decisions are described.
Agent policy is specified with objectives, constraints, acquisition/reward functions, stochastic sources, and fixed parameters (config or pseudocode).
Level of autonomy and human oversight are declared; interventions/overrides logged with frequency and impact.
Stopping criteria and anomaly/failure handling are defined; aborted runs/exclusions/post hoc corrections are reported.
Safety bounds, interlocks, shutdown mechanisms, and guardrails preventing unsafe experiments are described.
Traceable record of the campaign trajectory is maintained (logs or representative segments for statistical reproducibility).
Executable tutorials or notebooks illustrate key elements of data management, model development, and workflow integration.
Each tutorial/notebook links to the relevant manuscript sections and checklist items and includes versioning info.
Execution environment is documented (container image, lockfile, or environment spec) to mitigate dependency and API drift.
